implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: UI form for adding a new task, collects title, description, priority, category, and due date. It does not include a user name field.
implementation.name: TaskForm
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render a form with fields:
  - Title (required)
  - Description
  - Priority (Low / Medium / High)
  - Category (None / Work / Personal / Study)
  - Due Date
On submit:
  - Validate required fields
  - Call onSubmit with task data

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Handles submission from TaskForm and calls the backend API to create a task. Because the form does not collect a user name, none is included here.
implementation.name: handleCreateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
When TaskForm submits:
  - Call api.createTask with task data (no user name)
  - Reload tasks from backend
  - Close the form

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Makes POST request to /tasks with title, description, priority, category, due date, and completed flag. User name is not included.
implementation.name: api.createTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
Send POST request to /tasks with task data
IF response is OK:
  - Return created task
ELSE:
  - Throw error

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: FastAPI POST /tasks endpoint that receives task fields via TaskCreate model and passes them to database.add_task. User name is not included or validated.
implementation.name: create_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
On POST /tasks:
  - Receive TaskCreate payload
  - Call database.add_task with provided fields
  - Return created Task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Creates and persists a new task record to tasks.json. User name is not expected or stored.
implementation.name: add_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
Read current tasks
Create new Task with unique ID and provided fields
Append new task to list
Save list to tasks.json
Return new Task

missing_implementations: If any required field (title, description, priority, due date, or user name) is missing or empty, the task creation must fail, and an error must be returned indicating the missing field. Currently, description, due date, and user name are optional in the backend model (TaskBase) and no validation error is returned for missing description or due date. User name field is entirely absent from all layers.

A task is considered successfully created only if all five fields (title, description, priority, due date, and user name) are present and stored in the system. User name is not collected in the UI form, not passed from the frontend to the backend, not accepted or validated by the backend endpoint, and not stored by the database logic.
